steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $\sqrt{63y}$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Use the exponent rule
steps.content: A power of $\frac{1}{2}$ means square root. The exponent applies to the whole base $63y$: $$\left(63y\right)^{1/2}=\sqrt{63y}$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The correct answer is $\sqrt{63y}$, which is exactly equivalent to $(63y)^{1/2}$. C is correct.

steps.title: Eliminate the mismatches
steps.content: The answer with $63$ outside the radical is wrong. The answer with $y$ outside the radical is also wrong. Squaring the whole product before taking the square root would give $63y$, not $(63y)^{1/2}$.
